name: flagTelescopeReticles
description: Show or hide telescope reticle markers (crosshair symbols) on the sky at each connected telescope's current position.

name: flagTelescopeLabels
description: Show or hide telescope name labels next to each telescope reticle.

name: flagTelescopeCircles
description: Show or hide telescope field-of-view (FOV) circles. These circles represent the angular diameter visible through connected telescope eyepieces.

name: reticleColor
description: Color of telescope reticle markers. To read: use core.getStelProperty(). To set: use TelescopeControl.setReticleColor().

name: labelColor
description: Color of telescope name labels.

name: circleColor
description: Color of telescope field-of-view circles.

name: useTelescopeServerLogs
description: Enable or disable logging of telescope server communication. Logs are saved to log_TelescopeServer<slot>.txt in Stellarium's user directory.

name: useTelescopeServerExecutables
description: Use external telescope server executables instead of embedded servers. Requires serverExecutablesDirectoryPath to be set correctly.

name: serverExecutablesDirectoryPath
description: Directory path where telescope server executables (TelescopeServer*) are located. Used when useTelescopeServerExecutables is true.

name: setFlagTelescopeReticles
description: Shows or hides telescope reticle markers. Equivalent to setting the flagTelescopeReticles property.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to show reticles, false to hide

name: getFlagTelescopeReticles
description: Returns whether telescope reticle markers are currently visible.

name: setFlagTelescopeLabels
description: Shows or hides telescope name labels.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to show labels, false to hide

name: getFlagTelescopeLabels
description: Returns whether telescope name labels are currently visible.

name: setFlagTelescopeCircles
description: Shows or hides telescope field-of-view (FOV) circles.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to show FOV circles, false to hide

name: getFlagTelescopeCircles
description: Returns whether telescope FOV circles are currently visible.

name: setReticleColor
description: Sets the color of telescope reticle markers. Use new Color(r,g,b).toVec3f() to create the color vector.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values 0.0 to 1.0

name: getReticleColor
description: Returns the current color of telescope reticle markers.

name: getLabelColor
description: Returns the current color of telescope name labels.

name: setLabelColor
description: Sets the color of telescope name labels.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values 0.0 to 1.0

name: setCircleColor
description: Sets the color of telescope field-of-view circles.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values 0.0 to 1.0

name: getCircleColor
description: Returns the current color of telescope FOV circles.

name: setFontSize
description: Sets the font size for telescope name labels in pixels.

name: slewTelescopeToSelectedObject
description: Sends a GoTo command to the telescope at the specified slot to point at the currently selected celestial object. The telescope will physically move (if supported and connected).

parameters.name: idx
parameters.description: Telescope slot number (1-9, where 1 is the first telescope)

name: syncTelescopeWithSelectedObject
description: Syncs (aligns) the telescope at the specified slot with the currently selected celestial object. This updates the telescope's internal pointing model. Not all telescopes support this feature.
example:
// Align telescope #1 to the current view center
TelescopeControl.syncTelescopeWithSelectedObject(1);

name: slewTelescopeToViewDirection
description: Sends a GoTo command to the telescope at the specified slot to point at the center of the current screen view.
example:
// Point telescope #1 to wherever the screen is currently centered
TelescopeControl.slewTelescopeToViewDirection(1);

name: abortTelescopeSlew
description: Aborts the current slew (movement) command for the telescope at the specified slot. ⚠️ NOT ALL TELESCOPES SUPPORT THIS! A warning dialog may appear.
example:
// Stop the current movement of telescope #1
TelescopeControl.abortTelescopeSlew(1);

name: centeringScreenByTelescope
description: Centers the Stellarium screen view on the current position of the telescope at the specified slot. Useful for seeing what the telescope is pointing at.
example:
// Center screen on telescope #1's current position
TelescopeControl.centeringScreenByTelescope(1);

name: setFlagUseTelescopeServerLogs
description: Enables or disables logging of telescope server communication for debugging purposes. Equivalent to setting the useTelescopeServerLogs property.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to enable logging, false to disable

example:
// Enable debugging logs for telescope servers
TelescopeControl.setFlagUseTelescopeServerLogs(true);

name: getConnectedClientsNames
description: ⚠️ Returns a hash mapping slot numbers to telescope names for all currently connected telescopes.

name: isExistingClientAtSlot
description: ⚠️ Checks whether a telescope client exists (defined and active) at the specified slot.

name: isConnectedClientAtSlot
description: ⚠️ Checks whether the telescope client at the specified slot is currently connected to a telescope or server.

name: stopAllTelescopes
description: ⚠️ Stops all connected telescope clients and terminates any running server processes.

name: getDeviceModels
description: ⚠️ Returns a hash of available telescope device models (e.g., 'LX200', 'NexStar') with their properties (server name, default delay, etc.).
example:
var models = TelescopeControl.getDeviceModels();
core.debug("Available telescope models:", models.keys());
